_note: Synthetic GitHub `pull_request` event. Hand-authored: a GENUINE contributor who used the repo's PR template AND filled every section with real, specific content. Must NOT trip templateBody/intentMismatch/commitQuality — the structure is present AND substantive. Not captured from a real PR.

pull_request.title: Fix integer overflow in checksum for payloads > 2GB
pull_request.body:
## Summary

The CRC accumulator used a 32-bit signed int, so payloads over ~2GB wrapped negative and produced a wrong checksum (reported in #401).

## Changes Made

- Switch the accumulator in `src/checksum.js` to BigInt
- Mask back to 32 bits at the end so the wire format is unchanged
- Add `test/checksum_large.test.js` exercising a 2.5GB synthetic buffer via a stream stub

## Testing

New test fails on `main` (got -1247... ), passes here. Ran the full suite locally: 318 passing, no regressions.

## Checklist

- [x] Tests added
- [x] No public API / wire-format change

commit.message: Use BigInt accumulator to avoid 32-bit CRC overflow on >2GB payloads
Add large-payload regression test for checksum overflow
